_notes.warn_bytes: 10MB — a reasonable document upload. Above this starts to look like bulk export.
_notes.critical_bytes: 50MB — bulk data export. Multiple large files or a database dump.
_notes.philosophy: Cloud storage is normal. Only flag genuinely large transfers.

_notes.warn_bytes: 2MB — a few attachments. Normal personal email use.
_notes.critical_bytes: 10MB — bulk forwarding of files to personal email.
_notes.philosophy: Personal email during work hours is fine. Forwarding large files is not.

_notes.warn_bytes: 100KB to Pastebin/Hastebin etc — already concerning. These sites have almost no legitimate large-file use.
_notes.philosophy: Pastebin and similar have almost no legitimate large-transfer use in a law firm or HR company. Low thresholds.

_notes.warn_bytes: 5MB — file sharing via messaging. Could be legitimate.
_notes.critical_bytes: 20MB — bulk file transfer via messaging app.
_notes.philosophy: Messaging is common. Only flag when file transfer volume is very high.

_notes.warn_bytes: 50KB to a data broker — already worth noting.
_notes.critical_bytes: 200KB — bulk lookup or upload to data broker.
_notes.philosophy: Data brokers have very limited legitimate use. Low thresholds.

_notes.warn_bytes: 1KB to ngrok/webhook.site — flag immediately. No legitimate business use.
_notes.philosophy: Near-zero tolerance. Any significant traffic to these tools is a security incident.

behavior_rules.name: Normal cloud storage use
behavior_rules.description: Routine document uploads and downloads — expected business behavior.

behavior_rules.name: Large cloud storage transfer
behavior_rules.description: Volume consistent with bulk file export. Could be legitimate backup or data theft.
behavior_rules.message: Large file transfer detected to {domain} ({volume}). If this includes client data, please ensure it complies with your data handling policy.

behavior_rules.name: Critical cloud storage exfiltration
behavior_rules.description: Volume consistent with database export or bulk client file transfer.
behavior_rules.message: High-volume transfer to {domain} detected ({volume}). Your IT administrator has been notified.

behavior_rules.name: Personal email file forwarding
behavior_rules.description: Attachments being sent to personal email above normal volume.
behavior_rules.message: Elevated file transfer to personal email ({domain}) detected. Please ensure no client data is being forwarded.

behavior_rules.name: Pastebin / text dump
behavior_rules.description: Data being sent to a public text sharing service. Classic exfiltration vector.
behavior_rules.message: Data transfer to {domain} detected. Public paste sites are not approved for business data.

behavior_rules.name: Suspicious tool detected
behavior_rules.description: Traffic to tunnel, webhook capture, or port forwarding service. Near-zero tolerance.
behavior_rules.message: Suspicious network tool detected: {domain}. This has been flagged as a security incident.

behavior_rules.name: Data broker access
behavior_rules.description: Employee accessing a people-search or data broker site with significant data volume.
behavior_rules.message: Access to data broker service {domain} logged for compliance review.

behavior_rules.name: High-risk tool — risk weight multiplier
behavior_rules.description: Domains with risk_weight above 1.5 trigger at proportionally lower thresholds.
behavior_rules.message: Transfer to high-risk domain {domain} flagged for review.

behavior_rules.name: After-hours large transfer
behavior_rules.description: Large data transfer outside business hours (before 7am or after 8pm). Elevated concern.

cooldown._notes: Non-AI transfers get longer cooldowns — file transfers are naturally longer operations.

time_based_rules._notes: After-hours transfers trigger at 50% of the normal threshold. A 5MB upload to Dropbox at 11pm is more suspicious than the same upload at 2pm.
